_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- put in place continuous cyber and information security awareness and education programs, for example, training for specific groups
- use a range of methods to assess whether people’s security behaviour is improving
- compare the maturity of security awareness and culture in your organisation against others across government to determine effective improvements
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- incentivising the right behaviours across the whole organisation so that everyone knows how they contribute to security
- persuading senior leadership to represent a campaign personally
- communicating insights from previous incidents to ensure they are learned from
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting and reviewing Data Protection Impact Assessments, identifying processing activities that present high risk to individuals, and recommending proportionate technical and organizational measures to mitigate identified risks.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-003 (LS, cosine: 0.451), RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.449).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.2/10 (raw 0.523). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DDaT-only terms [across, against, assess, awareness, behaviour, behaviours] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, assessments, conducting, data, high, identified] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.828 (P=0.815, R=0.842). R exceeds P by 0.028 — the WIDAI KSA's 29 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 100 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (98.7%). "You can:
- put in place continuous cyber and information security awareness and" and "Skill in conducting and reviewing Data Protection Impact Assessments, identifyin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.470 < STS 0.523 (gap -0.054). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [across, against, assess] and [activities, assessments, conducting] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.3%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DDaT-only: [across, against, assess, awareness, behaviour, behaviours]. WIDAI-only: [activities, assessments, conducting, data, high, identified].
